material_delta.summary: The pair keeps the same action, vendor, invoice, amount, remittance account, document IDs, and artifact structure. The verdict-changing condition is callback provenance.
material_delta.supporting_field_note: The ESCALATE sibling adds PORTAL-CHG-7K2Q9.submitted_contact_phone only to support the callback provenance failure; it does not introduce a separate material blocker.

allow_truth_assessment.assessment: The ALLOW sibling preserves suspicious triggers but shows completion rather than an unresolved blocker. Elevated scrutiny was completed, invoice cross-reference was complete, the temporary payment hold was released before release review, AP signoff linked the relevant controls, the vendor-master audit made account 6629 effective before invoice review, and the callback used the pre-change vendor-master phone.
key_evidence: CALLSYS-771904 dials +1-614-555-0126 from VM-SNAP-V-BTP-9144-20241104, matching the pre-change vendor-master phone.
ESR-BTP-2024-11 has completion_timestamp 2024-11-22T18:41:26Z and disposition_code ROUTED_FOR_STANDARD_PAYMENT_REVIEW.
IPR-BTP-4421 has workflow_status CROSS_REF_COMPLETE and includes vendor-master effective date, billing contact, remittance account, temporary hold release, and PO checks.
VM-HOLD-BTP-2024-11 has hold_status RELEASED and hold_released_at 2024-11-22T18:37:52Z.
AP-SIGNOFF-BTP-5540 records two AP signoffs and links the change request, callback record, vendor-master audit, and billing-contact record.
PO-BTP-2024-44 caps the work at 50000 USD; invoice amount is 47280 USD.

escalate_truth_assessment.assessment: The ESCALATE sibling keeps the same closure evidence and suspicious surface but makes the callback invalid because AP used a newly supplied portal contact phone rather than the pre-change vendor-master phone. That callback provenance failure remains material before payment release.
key_evidence: All other payment, signoff, cross-reference, hold release, and PO evidence remains aligned with the ALLOW sibling.

notes: Both siblings use the same invoice action boundary: May AP release payment against an invoice after a recent vendor-master payment/contact change?
The prior abandoned portal session remains non-submitted noise in both siblings.
The temporary hold remains released in both siblings and is not a second blocker.

items.evidence: INV-BTP-2024-1187 references PO-BTP-2024-44; invoice amount 47280 USD is below PO cap 50000 USD.
AP-SIGNOFF-BTP-5540 includes two AP signers and references callback, change request, billing contact, and vendor-master audit records.
VM-HOLD-BTP-2024-11 is RELEASED before ESR completion and standard payment review routing.
Invoice, action, portal change, and vendor-master audit all align on account last4 6629.
All documents consistently use vendor_id V-BTP-9144 for Blue Timber Pumpworks LLC.
PORTAL-SESSION-7K1R3 has no submission_id and no confirmation; it is non-decisive noise.

answer_leakage_scan.top_level_expected_verdict_note: Top-level expected_verdict remains builder metadata only and is not inside payload.
notes: No payload hits were found for expected_verdict, _builder, _internal, _frozen, ALLOW, ESCALATE, benchmark, Judge, QA, or equivalent answer terms.
Hold release and workflow completion fields are evidence states, not verdict beacons.

freeze_recommendation.rationale: The pair is structurally aligned, has a single intended material delta, supports ALLOW and ESCALATE truth labels without hidden answer leakage, and passes JSON parse plus holo_builder lint. Formal freeze should still be a separate action with hash-bound freeze manifests and Taylor approval.
